standard_name: Abu Dhabi Healthcare Information and Cyber Security Standard (ADHICS)

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop, enforce and maintain a human resources security policy covering the security aspects of employment and termination.

criteria.text: Define management requirements on;
sub: Background verification for employees and contractors
Compliance with acceptable usage and other organizational Basic security policies

criteria.text: Mandate the requirements of non-disclosure and confidentiality during and after employment
Include reference to organizational disciplinary process

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall conduct background verification checks on all candidates for employment, contractors and third-party users.

criteria.text: Define background verification process addressing provisions of government mandates and entity demands
Establish criteria for background verification checks based on:

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity management shall ensure employees, contractors and third party users adopt and apply security in accordance with established entity policies and procedures.

criteria.text: Ensure employees, contractors and third party users are briefed on the entity’s information security compliance requirements Basic
Establish acceptable usage policy and ensure users read, accept and sign the policy prior to the provision of system, application or information access
Consider segregation of duties to avoid potential misuse of position or conflict of interest

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop new or modify existing awareness and training programs to include requirements of governmental and organizational information security demands.

criteria.text: Ensure employees, contractors and third party users are briefed on the entity’s information security compliance requirements Basic
Establish acceptable usage policy and ensure users read, accept and sign the policy prior to the provision of system, application or information access
Consider segregation of duties to avoid potential misuse of position or conflict of interest

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall conduct periodic security awareness campaigns, based on established yearly schedules.

criteria.text: Conduct awareness campaign for general and targeted user groups
Identify innovative methods and medium to communicate security requirements
Include incentive programs for user participation and adherence to security practices Basic The awareness campaign shall:
Present current risks around the work and industry, and ways to address
Present learning from incident
Demonstrates the need to protect healthcare information
Include benefit of information security compliance
Demonstrate stakeholder responsibilities
Highlight entity, government and regulatory demands

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish and enforce a disciplinary process for employees, where relevant contractors and third parties, who have committed security breaches.

criteria.text: Conduct awareness campaign for general and targeted user groups
Identify innovative methods and medium to communicate security requirements

Present current risks around the work and industry, and ways to address

Demonstrates the need to protect healthcare information
Include benefit of information security compliance
Demonstrate stakeholder responsibilities
Highlight entity, government and regulatory demands

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall define responsibilities concerning information security for performing employment termination or change of employment.

criteria.text: Establish internal and external communication protocol on employment
Ensure adequate knowledge transfers and responsibility handovers

controls.sub_domain: Termination or Change of Employment and Role
controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure recovery of all organizational assets upon termination of employment, contract or agreement.

criteria.text: Establish internal and external communication protocol on employment exit
Ensure adequate knowledge transfers and responsibility handovers

controls.sub_domain: Termination or Change of Employment and Role
controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall remove access rights and revoke privileges of individuals upon termination of employment, contract or agreement The healthcare entity shall remove access to systems, applications, information, secure areas, and work areas.

criteria.text: Establish internal and external communication protocol on employment exit

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop, implement and maintain an asset management policy to:.

criteria.text: Establish framework to effectively manage the entity’s information assets through ownership assignment, accountability & responsibility definition, recording and maintaining of all/relevant properties of asset
Define roles and responsibilities for actions expected out of asset management policy, and shall have functional KPI’s for business/function leaders
Define and enforce classification schemes, as applicable for AD Health Sector (Public, Restricted, Confidential & Secret)
Identify requirements of data retention, handling and disposal
Have provisions to manage Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) arrangements
Be reviewed, updated and maintained at planned intervals or during significant changes to operating or risk environment, whichever is earlier
Be approved by the entity’s top management or its head and shall be communicated to all employees and third parties having a role in care delivery

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall have all their information assets identified, recorded and maintained through an information asset inventory.

criteria.text: The inventory can be centralized or distributed (function/line-of- business/service wise) based on the entity’s internal structures, and shall be updated

The inventory shall establish the relations between various types of information assets, in support of care delivery; Sample illustration: Service A  needs B Information  supplied by C Device/Equipment/Process/Dependent-Service  processed using D Application (ERP/EMR/Office Automation Applications/etc.)  running on E Technology (server/systems)  supported/operated/managed by XYZ Roles (human resources involved in care delivery)

controls.control_demand: Ownership for each identified assets shall be assigned to a designated role:.

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish and enforce rules on the acceptable use of information assets:.

criteria.text: Entities shall maintain records of user acceptance on the acceptable use of information assets The rule shall consider general requirements and industry best practices and shall have management requirements to reduce probabilities of information leakage/loss/theft and system compromises.

controls.control_demand: Entity management shall be aware of emerging cyber risks, and shall address risk due to the exploitation of the concept-in-practice “Bring Your Own Device (BYOD)”.

criteria.text: The rules shall be communicated to all employees and contractors in support of care delivery, and shall be read and acknowledged by all Basic

Entities shall maintain records of user acceptance on the acceptable use of information assets The rule shall consider general requirements and industry best practices and shall have management requirements to reduce probabilities of information leakage/loss/theft and system compromises.

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall classify all information assets, that categorises information assets into one of the following classification scheme:  Public  Restricted  Confidential  Secret.

Information classification shall consider value of the information and shall be more restrictive/deterrent based on the entity’s tolerance of financial impact Transitional due to compromise of the information considered.
The level of essential protection needed for an asset shall be considered while Transitional determining asset classification.
The healthcare entity shall establish process to reassess and/or change information classification, based on the following:.

criteria.text: Changes to environment (location, access, storage, processing, usage, etc.)
Changes in protection levels

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish process to interpret classification schemes, while receiving information from other entities/3rd parties and shall apply all Transitional essential control measures to safeguard/protect against compromise.

criteria.text: Change in the value of information Transitional
Changes to environment (location, access, storage, processing, usage, etc.)
Changes in protection levels

controls.control_demand: Handling procedures shall be defined for information, consistent with their classification.

criteria.text: Security requirements based on asset value shall be considered in the handling procedures

controls.control_demand: Ensure adoption and application of handling procedures while handling

criteria.text: Handling procedures shall detail security requirements during:  Access granting and privilege allocation  Processing Basic  Storing  Communication/sharing  Printing
Security requirements based on asset value shall be considered in the handling procedures

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall manage removable media in accordance with the classification scheme, handling procedures and acceptable use of assets.

criteria.text: Handling procedures shall detail security requirements during:  Access granting and privilege allocation  Processing Basic  Storing  Communication/sharing  Printing
Security requirements based on asset value shall be considered in the handling procedures

controls.control_demand: Access and usage of removable media shall be controlled and shall be based on the entity’s management approval.

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish medical devices and equipment management procedures for each category of identified medical devices and Basic equipment.

controls.control_demand: Access and privilege allocation for medical devices shall be provided to defined roles, with essential qualification and experience required to operate.
The healthcare entity shall prevent unauthorized disclosure, modification, destruction or loss of patient health information stored on medical devices and equipment.

criteria.text: Information stored within the medical devices and equipment shall be encrypted Transitional
Electronic communication between medical devices and equipment shall be encrypted
Healthcare entities shall define minimum essential qualification required to operate and/or handle medical devices and equipment
Copies of valuable health data is moved to a secure storage/location to reduce the risk of its data damage or loss

controls.control_demand: Healthcare facilities shall consider wired communication facility for medical devices and equipment. Usage of wireless communication facility with Transitional medical devices and equipment shall be avoided to the extent possible.

criteria.text: Information stored within the medical devices and equipment shall be encrypted Transitional
Electronic communication between medical devices and equipment shall be encrypted
Healthcare entities shall define minimum essential qualification required to operate and/or handle medical devices and equipment

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall dispose information assets, when no longer required:  by the entity Basic  on basis of regulatory demands  for legal proceedings.
The healthcare entity shall establish a control process that ensures data once destroyed is not recovered.
Media, both digital and physical, when no longer required shall be destroyed by the entity.
The healthcare entity shall establish control procedures for the secure disposal or reuse of media, equipment, devices and systems, containing classified information.

criteria.text: Ensure sensitive data and licensed software has been securely removed beyond recovery, prior to disposal

controls.control_demand: system shall be verified and complied with, prior to disposal.

criteria.text: Ensure sensitive data and licensed software has been securely removed beyond recovery, prior to disposal

controls.control_demand: All disposal requirement shall be authorized by entity management prior to disposal.

criteria.text: Ensure sensitive data and licensed software has been securely removed beyond recovery, prior to disposal

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall maintain records, on media disposal. The records shall have, but not be limited to, the following fields:  Information and/or asset owner  Type of media  Classification Advanced  Disposal type  Reason for disposal  Retention expiry date (if data)  Data removal confirmation and evidence  Disposal authorized by 3. Physical and Environmental Security Information and information processing equipment(s)/facilities has greater dependence on physical environment to achieve business objectives. Physical environment and its security are foundational elements to define secure data processing, data storage, data communication/sharing, data hosting and data disposal. Physical and environmental security programs and efforts define the various measures or controls that protect healthcare entities from loss of connectivity, availability of information processing facilities, storage (backup and archival) equipment(s)/facilities and medical equipment’s/devices caused by theft, fire, flood, intentional destruction, unintentional damage, mechanical failure, power failure, etc.

criteria.text: Ensure sensitive data and licensed software has been securely removed beyond recovery, prior to disposal

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop, implement and maintain a physical and environmental security policy, to ensure adequate physical and environmental protection of entities information assets.

criteria.text: Be relevant and appropriate for entities operational and risk environment, concerning internal and external threats
Address requirements of secure storage of hazardous or combustible materials that ensures avoidance of:  human injuries or loss of life  damage to information and information systems Basic
Consider classification of information assets and their physical presence
Define roles and responsibilities for actions expected out of physical and environmental security policy
Be reviewed, updated and maintained at planned intervals or during significant changes to operating or risk environment, whichever is earlier
Be read and formally acknowledged by all users
Be approved by entity’s top management or head of the entity, and shall be communicated to all employees and third parties having role in care delivery

criteria.text: Establish access procedures to loading and unloading areas to restrict Basic access to only authorized personnel
Inspect and register incoming and outgoing materials, in accordance with healthcare entity’s asset management procedures

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall identify and apply security measures to protect equipment, medical devices and information processing systems while off-.

criteria.text: Ensure manufacturer’s recommendation and instructions are followed, while equipment, medical devices and information processing systems are off-site Advanced
Ensure that movement and possession (chain of custody) logs for off-site equipment, medical devices and information processing systems maintained and verified
Ensure security measures are applied to protect off-site equipment, medical devices and information processing systems from probabilities of information leakage, tampering and unauthorized activities

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure that unattended equipment, medical devices and information processing systems are protected against information leakage and unauthorized activities.

criteria.text: Ensure manufacturer’s recommendation and instructions are followed, while equipment, medical devices and information processing systems are off-site Advanced
Ensure that movement and possession (chain of custody) logs for off-site equipment, medical devices and information processing systems maintained and verified
Ensure security measures are applied to protect off-site equipment, medical devices and information processing systems from probabilities of information leakage, tampering and unauthorized activities

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall define and enforce a clear desk and clear screen policy to paper documents, removable storage media, and information processing systems.

criteria.text: Ensure manufacturer’s recommendation and instructions are followed, while equipment, medical devices and information processing systems are off-site Advanced
Ensure that movement and possession (chain of custody) logs for off-site equipment, medical devices and information processing systems maintained and verified

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall implement a formal user registration and de- registration process.

criteria.text: Ensure request for user registration and de-registration are process driven, and are in compliance with established criteria for access
Ensure unique user accounts are created for each individual requiring access, and prohibit sharing of same account with multiple users
Ensure group user account are not created or used
Revoke user accounts during employee exit
Revalidate access requirements during role change
Maintain records/list of persons authorised to use healthcare entity’s information systems, applications, medical devices and equipment
Establish and follow separate user registration and de-registration process for temporary and third party user account requirements

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall restrict and control allocation of privileges, based on principles of need to know.

criteria.text: Ensure request for user registration and de-registration are process driven, and are in compliance with established criteria for access
Ensure unique user accounts are created for each individual requiring access, and prohibit sharing of same account with multiple users

Maintain records/list of persons authorised to use healthcare entity’s information systems, applications, medical devices and equipment
Establish and follow separate user registration and de-registration process for temporary and third party user account requirements

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish process for secure allocation, use and management of security credentials.

criteria.text: Ensure default application and system passwords are changed and not being used
Ensure passwords are always hashed and stored in encrypted format
During initial user account creation, communicate details of user account and password in two different communication modalities
Enforce complexity requirements on password characters, and shall have at least:
sub: One number, one upper-case and lower-case character, and a special character

criteria.text: Enforce passwords, including that of service accounts and privileged accounts, are recycled at an entity-defined time frame
Ensure that password history is maintained, and shall restrict users from using immediately used previous passwords (at least 3 previous passwords)
Educate users to adopt good practices while selecting and using passwords

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall protect confidential and secret information on portable or removable media, mobile or portable devices, and medical equipment or devices.

criteria.text: Authenticate user, where relevant, access to equipment, devices and
Ensure media containing confidential and secret information is password protected and encrypted Transitional
Where relevant, control access to medical equipment and devices through password enforcement in compliance with the healthcare entities password complexity and usage requirements
Control access to mobile and portable devices hosting confidential and secret information
Establish mobile device management process to protect entity information being used, processed or stored in mobile devices

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall control access to equipment, devices, system and facilities at teleworking sites.

criteria.text: Authenticate user, where relevant, access to equipment, devices and media
Ensure media containing confidential and secret information is password protected and encrypted Transitional
Where relevant, control access to medical equipment and devices through password enforcement in compliance with the healthcare entities password complexity and usage requirements
Control access to mobile and portable devices hosting confidential and secret information
Establish mobile device management process to protect entity information being used, processed or stored in mobile devices

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall review access and privileges granted to its user.

criteria.text: Establish process for the reviewing of user access and associated privileges to various entity resources
Define responsibility for access and privileges review, based on entity resources being accessed
Conduct user access and privileges review at least once a year or earlier, as required by the entity’s risk environment
Maintain an up to date inventory of access granted and privileges assigned
Define the criteria for the automatic revocation of user access and privileges based on the entity’s defined period of inactivity or non-usage of resources

controls.control_demand: Access to the entity’s network and network services shall be controlled, and shall be provided based on specific need for which the user is authorized for.
The healthcare entity shall use appropriate authentication methods to control access of remote users.

criteria.text: Ensure all remote login and access are only through secure channels
Consider usage of multifactor authentication scheme to control access by remote users

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall identify all equipment and devices connected to its network, and shall have automated mechanism to detect unauthorized Basic equipment and devices.

criteria.text: Ensure all remote login and access are only through secure channels

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall define and implement network routing controls to ensure information flow and system, devices, equipment connections are not compromised and are in line with requirements of Access Control Policy.

criteria.text: Establish processes for secure configuration and rules application for network routing requirements
Always ensure source and destination address and services or ports are used while defining and applying routing rules
Enable routing protection countermeasures to avoid manipulation of routing systems and tables Transitional
Define and implement network architecture that segregates and isolates internal and publically accessible systems.
External connections to information systems and networks outside entity shall be managed through interfaces consisting of perimeter protection devices (such as firewalls)
Ensure that communications with external systems, networks and key internal systems are always monitored for malicious and suspicious payloads
Periodically scan for any covert channel connections to public networks bypassing entity security defence

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure wireless access within the entity is secured.

criteria.text: Establish processes for secure configuration and rules application for network routing requirements

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall establish and enforce secure log-on and log-off procedures to control access to system and applications.

criteria.text: Ensure that access to entities systems, applications and services that process, use or store healthcare information are authenticated
Enforce automated locking of workstation/system after a predefined period of inactivity
Establish authorization procedures, based on classification of systems, Basic application, services and information in scope
Establish and enforce idle session time-out requirements
Automatically terminate inactive sessions after a predefined period of session inactivity

Display a logon banner that requires the user to acknowledge and accept security terms and their responsibilities before access to the system is granted The healthcare entity shall create unique identifier (user ID) for each users who AC 6.2 require access to entities systems, applications or services, and shall implement a suitable authentication technique. Grant each user with a unique identifier
Ensure all user activities are logged with the associated identifier

Enforce role-based access mechanisms

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall protect its information assets from malware.

criteria.text: Ensure minimum security configurations is maintained in all information assets, as applicable and as relevant
Implement anti-malware and anti-virus protection mechanisms for network and individual information systems (server, workstation, mobile/portable computing devices)
Ensure anti-malware and anti-virus protections mechanisms are updated and current
Enable real-time protection capabilities
Establish and enforce periodic scan schedules
Scan removable media for viruses and malware on all occasions when they are connected to information systems
Disable auto-run features for removable media on information systems
Configure anti-malware and anti-virus protection systems to alert responsible stakeholders on event, incident or anomaly detection
Provide ongoing awareness for users on techniques, tactics and procedure to avoid and minimize probabilities of malware and virus attacks

criteria.text: Implement data leakage prevention (DLP) measures

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall conduct periodic independent assessment to ensure information assets are secure and always protected.

criteria.text: Establish yearly schedules and conduct:
sub: Penetration testing on the entity’s system, network, applications and security infrastructures and environment
Vulnerability assessment on all entity’s system, network, applications and security infrastructures and environment
Web security assessments on web applications accessible over internet

criteria.text: Establish processes to conduct security testing and authorization by Transitional authorized business and security stakeholders for new system/application and upgrades prior to production roll-out and usage
Establish processes to mitigate and manage identified findings and vulnerabilities
Share reports on identified findings and vulnerabilities and the status of mitigation with:
Periodically follow up on the progress and status of mitigation measures with the appropriate stakeholders

Verify effectiveness and efficiency of mitigation measures The healthcare entity shall ensure that assessment data is not available with OM 7.2 third parties engaged to conduct assessments beyond the time of engagement The healthcare entity shall:
Ensure that system, network, applications and security related information is shared with third parties when they are on-site Transitional

Ensure that all information related to the entity’s system, network, applications and security infrastructures and environment and assessment outcomes are erased from the involved third party’s assets and environment after the completion of the assessment activity

Ensure that any shared reports are suitably protected through an adequate encryption mechanism Abu Dhabi Government’s vision towards modernization and enhancement of society and services necessitates sharing of appropriate information with eligible stakeholders within and across entities. Stakeholder utilization of information from within and across entity has influenced decisions, and improved outcomes. It is essential that the communication between various information processing components are provisioned through controlled communication process and channel. It is essential healthcare entities define criteria, rules and controls that secure communication processes, components, interfaces, channels and stakeholders to securely aid human to machine, machine to machine, machine to human and human to human communication to facilitate information exchange. Risk environment of the connected world demands that an entity’s management be conscious of the current risk environment concerning communication and information exchange, and that it defines proactive measures that shall:  Secure entities communication infrastructures  Ensure information exchange are controlled through formal exchange agreements and controls  Ensure information is delivered to right stakeholders or information processing components

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall develop, enforce and maintain a health information protection policy that ensures management’s commitment to protect healthcare information.

criteria.text: Define management requirements on;
sub: Criteria for access and acceptable usage

criteria.text: Mandate the requirements of non-disclosure and confidentiality during and after employment
Define government sanctions and legal obligations
Include reference to organizational disciplinary process

controls.control_demand: The healthcare entity shall ensure that healthcare information under its custody is suitably protected.

criteria.text: Conduct orientation on healthcare information protection and sanctions to all its employees, relevant contractors and third parties prior to their access to healthcare information
Establish stricter process to ensure clear desk and clear screen practices are adhered to in areas where healthcare information is used, processed or handled
Define and enforce criteria for healthcare information access

Ensure access to health information systems and applications are restricted for individuals possessing a valid licence to practice their profession within the UAE, and any exception shall be authorized by entity CISO based on adequate justification
Control and restrict privileges for printing and sharing of healthcare Basic information
Ensure cleaning staff access to areas where patient related healthcare information is being viewed, accessed, used, processed, stored and/or destroyed are monitored or under surveillance coverage
Ensure any hardcopy/media containing healthcare information is shredded after its usefulness
Establish processes for shredding all hardcopy documents before their disposal
Ensure that healthcare information with personal identifiers is not available unattended
Ensure printing of healthcare information is limited to local printers and are not printed through uncontrolled network printers
Establish processes to notify the health sector regulator of any probabilities of breaches involving healthcare information

Third Party Security Operational efficiency, time to deliver and cost saving aspects compels entity management to utilize third party services or resources to complement service delivery. Involvement of third parties in the process of care delivery and associated areas are inevitable and needs stronger control measures to secure entity assets and information. Healthcare entity management shall be cognizant of the fact that a significant portion of privacy breaches originates with organizations that contracted activities and services to third parties. Adequate due diligence to activities and services to be contracted, and a proactive identification and definition of control environment to secure privacy and information assets would minimize damages and benefit healthcare entities and the government. Entities that entrust access to third parties acknowledge and share responsibilities for the breaches. A healthcare entity’s management shall be aware of the risk environment related to third party services and resources, establish a suitable framework for third party management and define a control environment that shall:  Reduce probabilities of information leakage and loss  Secure information assets  Minimize unauthorized access and usage  Uphold organizational and governmental reputation  Ensure service continuity Objective: To ensure third party services are controlled through suitable procedural obligations and contractual terms to secure privacy and protect information assets.
